name: compliance
description: Cross-cutting compliance substrate. Owns eight entities: ConsentCategory (config-backed catalogue: essential / functional / marketing / analytics / ai-training / minor-parental), ConsentRecord (immutable subject × category × decision snapshots — the audit-material consent evidence), Dsar (data-subject-request state machine: received → triaging → collecting → assembling → delivered | rejected), DsarArtefact (per-module contribution to a DSAR bundle — points at a storage::File), LegalHold (freezes retention on a subject or record set while litigation / audit is active), RetentionRun (audit trail of every retention sweep: what was purged, what was held, per-tenant), Subprocessor (versioned VPC registry — every third party that receives subject data), SafeguardingIncident (minor-safeguarding report inbox distinct from audit — domain-driven, not diff-driven). Ships the `HasConsent`, `IsRetentionAware`, `HasLegalHold`, `IsDsarContributor` traits + the `#[DsarExportable]` / `#[DsarErasable]` / `#[ConsentRequired]` / `#[RetentionPolicy]` / `#[LegalHoldable]` attributes so downstream modules opt into the DSAR / retention / consent-gate machinery without importing the SDK. Not a per-module obligation — the DSAR runner, retention runner, legal-hold policy, and consent-gate facade all live here; every other module is a contributor via events + attributes.

attribute_driven.description: Modules opt in to compliance orchestration via attributes. Discovery pass at boot compiles the DsarContributorRegistry + RetentionPolicyResolver + LegalHoldGate registries so runtime is a hash-map lookup, never reflection.

attributes.DsarExportable: Marks a model class as DSAR-exportable. `#[DsarExportable(subject: 'owner_id', include: ['*'], exclude: ['password_hash'])]`. The DsarOrchestrator scans registered classes when assembling a subject's export bundle. Params: subject (required, column name pointing at the subject id), include (glob of columns — default `['*']`), exclude (columns to redact), format (json | csv — default `json`).
attributes.DsarErasable: Marks a model class as DSAR-erasable. `#[DsarErasable(subject: 'owner_id', strategy: 'anonymize', retain_shell: true)]`. Strategy: `hard_delete` = row removed, `anonymize` = subject-identifying columns hashed, `soft_delete` = deleted_at set. `retain_shell`: keep a stub row for audit reconciliation.
attributes.ConsentRequired: Marks an action or class as gated on a specific consent category. `#[ConsentRequired(category: 'marketing')]` on a MarketingEmailJob refuses dispatch when the recipient has no active ConsentRecord for that category. Enforced by the ConsentGate facade + compliance.consent.gate middleware.
attributes.RetentionPolicy: Class-level, declares retention behaviour when the module's own retention.json is not authoritative. `#[RetentionPolicy(hot_days: 30, cold_days: 365, on_purge: 'anonymize')]`. Overrides the RetentionPolicyResolver default when the model belongs to a tenant whose plan tier supports overrides.
attributes.LegalHoldable: Marks a class as legal-hold-eligible. `#[LegalHoldable(subject: 'owner_id')]`. Rows on legal-held subjects are frozen from retention purges + soft-deletes (soft-delete still allowed for user control; hard-delete refused with `LegalHoldActiveException`).

consent_architecture.description: Consent is per-subject × per-category, snapshotted at every state change. Never mutated — withdrawal writes a new row with decision=withdrawn. The active decision is `latest row per (subject_id, category_key)`.
categories_default: essential (always granted; cannot be withdrawn without deleting the account)
analytics (aggregated + anonymised product analytics; separate from functional)
ai_training (allow subject data to be included in AI model training corpuses)
minor_parental (COPPA / FERPA parental consent for under-13 / under-16 subjects)
consent_architecture.gate_evaluation: ConsentGate::has($subjectId, $categoryKey) reads the latest ConsentRecord row and returns bool. Cached in Redis with 5m TTL keyed on `compliance:consent:{subject}:{category}` — invalidated on ConsentGiven / ConsentWithdrawn events.
consent_architecture.minor_flow: When ConsentCategory has `requires_guardian=true`, ConsentRecord.decision transitions require a companion row where guardian_user_id is set + evidence (verification method + timestamp) captured. Enforced by ConsentRecordObserver.

dsar_orchestration.description: DSAR is a state machine coordinating cross-module read + write. One Dsar row per request. Multiple DsarArtefact rows (one per contributing module).

sla_days.note: GDPR Art. 12(3) baseline is 30d; extension up to 60d for complex requests must be communicated within the first month. Enterprise contracts may negotiate SLAs; overrides live in tenancy::TenantSetting.compliance.dsar_sla_days.

dsar_orchestration.contributor_discovery: DsarContributorRegistry scans #[DsarExportable] + #[DsarErasable] classes at boot. Each contributor implements `Academorix\Compliance\Contracts\DsarContributor` with `collect(SubjectId $s): Iterable` + `erase(SubjectId $s): void`.

retention_orchestration.description: RetentionRunner is the ONLY canonical purge orchestrator. Every module declares its per-entity retention windows in `modules/<name>/retention.json`. The runner reads them all + respects LegalHold + applies tenant-tier overrides.
retention_orchestration.runner_schedule: Nightly 03:00 tenant-local time (per tenancy::TenantSetting.timezone). Long tenancies partitioned into hourly slots to avoid thundering herd on the compliance-mail queue.
retention_orchestration.legal_hold_precedence: LegalHoldGate::isHeld($subject, $context) short-circuits every purge candidate. Held rows are counted in the RetentionRun.held_count column but never touched.

legal_hold_policy.description: LegalHold overrides normal retention. Applied at the SUBJECT level (any user_id) or the SCOPE level (all rows in a tenant, all rows tagged with a case id).
scopes: subject (single subject_id — blocks their DSAR erasure + retention purges)
tenant (entire tenant — blocks retention runner + tenant-erasure cascade)
case (records tagged with case_ref — flexible, driven by litigation case number)
class (an entire model class — rare, used for regulatory inquiries requiring full-table freeze)
legal_hold_policy.duration: Explicit expires_at required unless authorised by super_admin. Auto-release via ExpireLegalHoldsJob when expired.
legal_hold_policy.acknowledgement: Applying a LegalHold requires two-person approval (applied_by + approved_by). Enforced by LegalHoldObserver.creating hook.
legal_hold_policy.audit_trail: Every apply / release / expiry writes an entry into audit::AuditLog with actor + reason + scope.

subprocessor_registry.description: Versioned catalogue of every third party receiving subject data. Feed rendered as public JSON at `GET /compliance/subprocessors.json` for the Trust Center + as PDF for DPAs.
subprocessor_registry.versioning: Every material change to a subprocessor (new region, new data class, new purpose) writes a new SubprocessorVersion row + fires SubprocessorUpdated. Historical versions immutable.
subprocessor_registry.public_feed: Rendered by SubprocessorFeedRenderer. Cache-Control 1h. Change to the feed dispatches SubprocessorChangedNotification to every tenant's DPO contact.

safeguarding_flow.description: Minor-safeguarding reports (concerns about a minor's welfare). Distinct from audit (which is diff-driven); distinct from activity (which is user-visible feed). Reports go into a triaged inbox with escalation SLAs.
severity: concern (single flag, review within 5 business days)
urgent (multiple flags OR keyword match, review within 24h)
critical (immediate risk, escalate to tenant safeguarding officer + external authority per jurisdiction)
safeguarding_flow.external_referrals: critical incidents surface a `pending_external_referral` flag; the tenant's designated safeguarding lead completes the referral out-of-band + records the referral reference. Compliance module never contacts external authorities automatically.

breach_detection.description: Signal fanout, not primary detection. Compliance subscribes to signals from storage (FileVirusFound), audit (unusual mass-export patterns), tenants (impossible-travel logins if / when Auth module lands), and produces `BreachDetected` events that feed the platform-admin breach dashboard.
breach_detection.notification_deadline_reference: GDPR Art. 33 (72h supervisory authority notification) + Art. 34 (data-subject notification without undue delay when high risk).
breach_detection.tenants_receive: The tenant's DPO TenantContact receives the BreachDetectedNotification within 15 minutes of the platform-admin acknowledging the incident. Subject-facing notification is orchestrated but NEVER auto-sent — requires human approval in the platform-admin dashboard.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant compliance oversight. DSAR review + subprocessor management + legal-hold + breach dashboard.

central.description: Public-facing compliance surface. Subprocessor feed (Trust Center) + DSAR download redemption (signed URL). No authentication — signature IS the credential.
